name: ALMA CCI Momentum Exhaustion Pullback
edge_hypothesis: ALMA (Arnaud Legoux Moving Average) uses a Gaussian-weighted kernel centered near the most recent price, giving it adaptive smoothing with minimal lag — similar to KAMA's speed-adaptation edge but using a fundamentally different algorithm. KAMA adapts via efficiency ratio; ALMA adapts via Gaussian offset parameter. Since KAMA-based strategies are the #2 highest PPR family (PPR 4.268 for kama_stoch_pullback_v1), testing ALMA as an alternative adaptive baseline explores whether the speed-adaptation edge generalizes beyond KAMA. CCI measures price deviation from its statistical mean — when CCI crosses above -100 from below, it signals the end of a bearish momentum extreme and the start of a bullish momentum reset. This is NOT a mean-reversion play (fading extremes) but a momentum-exhaustion play: entering as selling pressure exhausts and momentum begins to shift. The combination of ALMA trend direction + CCI exhaustion timing should capture entries at the precise moment a pullback completes within an adaptive trend.

variants.name: alma_cci_exhaustion_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core thesis: ALMA adaptive trend + CCI momentum exhaustion from -100 zone. 8:1 R:R (12 ATR TP / 1.5 ATR stop). CCI crossing above -100 signals bearish exhaustion in a bullish ALMA trend. Only 2 entry conditions (within max-2 rule).

components.notes: Arnaud Legoux MA — Gaussian-weighted adaptive smoothing. Period 9, offset 6.0, sigma 0.85. Provides trend direction with minimal lag.
Commodity Channel Index — crossing above -100 signals bearish momentum exhaustion. Entry times pullback completion within ALMA trend.
ATR-based position sizing and 8:1 R:R framework.

variants.name: alma_cci_exhaustion_v1_10to1
variants.description: Extended TP variant: 15 ATR TP for 10:1 R:R. Tests whether ALMA's adaptive trend holds long enough for wider profit targets. If ALMA identifies sustained trends better than fixed-period MAs, wider TP should capture more of the move without increasing whipsaw.

components.notes: ALMA adaptive trend direction — wider TP tests trend persistence.
CCI momentum exhaustion entry from -100 zone.

variants.name: alma_cci_exhaustion_v1_relaxed_8to1
variants.description: Relaxed CCI threshold: crossing above -80 (instead of -100). This captures shallower pullbacks where momentum hasn't fully reached extreme exhaustion. Tests whether moderate CCI pullbacks into ALMA trend generate enough trades with sufficient edge. Historical pattern: relaxed thresholds increase trade count at the cost of per-trade conviction.

components.notes: Relaxed CCI threshold (-80/80) for higher trade frequency on 4h.

indicator_roles.baseline: ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 — Gaussian-weighted adaptive MA, provides trend direction with minimal lag via offset centering
indicator_roles.confirmation: CCI_20_0.015 — measures price deviation from statistical mean, crossing from extreme zones times momentum exhaustion entries
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework
